original_hypothesis.title: USDT deprecate() upgrade switch can redirect token logic to malicious contract causing sudden operational breakage for Router/Aggregator integrations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TetherToken has an application-level upgrade mechanism: owner can set deprecated=true and upgradedAddress, after which transfer/transferFrom/approve/allowance/balanceOf/totalSupply forward into upgradedAddress. If USDT is used in Router/Aggregator flows, deprecate() can instantly change transfer semantics (reverts, blacklisting, fee changes, non-standard behavior) and break deposits, router migrations (approve patterns), or any component expecting legacy behavior.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is not a generic 'owner rug' claim: the concrete failure mode is systemic integration outage because Router/Aggregator use low-level calls and allowance/accounting assumptions. Needs operational validation of whether USDT is a supported asset and how Bifrost/routers handle it under deprecation.
deployment_assumptions: USDT (this legacy TetherToken) is actually used as a supported asset in the protocol environment
USDT owner triggers deprecate() to an upgraded contract with changed/hostile semantics, or upgradedAddress is misconfigured
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment evidence of USDT usage/addresses in provided scripts; this is an in-scope contract but may be test-only in this repository.
preconditions: Token contract is treated as USDT-equivalent by protocol operators and integrated into Router flows
Owner can and does flip deprecated flag

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: ERC20 behavior can change at runtime without proxy standards, impacting all downstream integrations

operational_questions: Is this USDT contract actually deployed/used, or just a test artifact?
If used, does Bifrost/Router restrict supported assets to canonical tokens only?
What are the runbooks for handling token deprecations/blacklists/pauses?
validation_requirements: Confirm whether USDT is in asset whitelist
Simulate deprecate() and observe Router deposit/transferOut/_routerDeposit behavior
Review monitoring for sudden token behavior changes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Owner deprecates token to upgraded contract with altered semantics (e.g., revert on transferFrom or tax transfers)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Trigger normal Router/Aggregator operations that call transferFrom/approve/transfer
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Observe systemic failures: stuck funds, failed migrations, halted deposits/outbounds
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Owner action (external to attacker under standard threat model)

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Not a takeover of THORChain contracts, but can effectively disable USDT flows
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: High: deposits/outbounds/migrations for USDT could halt or mis-account
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium/High depending on ability to pause USDT support and migrate balances
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Asset support, vault churn/migration, user deposits

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Deprecate(upgradedAddress) event; changed behavior in transfer calls
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Sudden spike in failed USDT deposits/approvals/migrations

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
